title: Implementation placed in book_api/ subdir, root app.py is empty
evidence: app.py is 0 bytes; real code in book_api/app.py. _agent_stdout.log shows write_file to root app.py was refused as a 'sensitive system path'.
suggestion: None needed — scorers found and tested book_api/ (test_coverage=0.98). Note the harness path restriction for cross-run comparison.

title: POST/PUT echo client-supplied JSON verbatim in the response body
evidence: book_api/app.py:44 returns {'id': book_id, **data} and :91 returns {'id': id, **data} — extra/unknown keys the client sends are reflected back rather than the persisted row.
suggestion: Return the row read back from the DB so the response reflects stored state (e.g. missing year/isbn default to null).

title: PUT relies on rowcount for 404 and does not read back persisted values
evidence: book_api/app.py:88 uses cursor.rowcount==0 to signal 'not found'; test_update_book (test_app.py:109) covers the happy path but no test asserts 404 on PUT to a missing id.
suggestion: Add a test for PUT /books/{id} against a non-existent id expecting 404.
